---
title: "Scenario: Single-cell RNA-seq Analysis"
description: >
  Version-controlled scRNA-seq workflow with QC checkpoints and lineage tracking
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

Single-cell RNA-seq analysis is inherently iterative: QC thresholds, clustering resolutions, and cell type annotations are revised multiple times. OmicsLake captures each decision point, enabling comparison and rollback.

## Setup

```r
library(OmicsLake)
lake <- Lake$new("scrna_project")
```

## Step 1: Import Raw Data

```r
# Simulated scRNA-seq count matrix (genes × cells)
set.seed(42)
n_genes <- 2000
n_cells <- 5000
raw_matrix <- matrix(rpois(n_genes * n_cells, lambda = 2),
                     nrow = n_genes,
                     dimnames = list(paste0("Gene_", 1:n_genes),
                                     paste0("Cell_", 1:n_cells)))

# Cell-level metadata
cell_meta <- data.frame(
  cell_id = colnames(raw_matrix),
  n_genes = colSums(raw_matrix > 0),
  total_counts = colSums(raw_matrix),
  pct_mito = runif(n_cells, 0, 25)
)

lake$put("raw_counts", as.data.frame(raw_matrix))
lake$put("cell_metadata", cell_meta)
lake$snap("01_raw")
```

## Step 2: QC --- Explore Different Thresholds

```r
# Strict QC
lake$ref("cell_metadata") |>
  dplyr::filter(n_genes > 500, total_counts > 1000, pct_mito < 10) |>
  save_as("qc_strict_cells", lake)
# How many cells pass?
nrow(lake$get("qc_strict_cells"))

lake$snap("02_qc_strict")

# Lenient QC
lake$ref("cell_metadata") |>
  dplyr::filter(n_genes > 200, total_counts > 500, pct_mito < 20) |>
  save_as("qc_lenient_cells", lake)
nrow(lake$get("qc_lenient_cells"))

lake$snap("02_qc_lenient")
```

Both QC strategies are preserved. You can compare cell counts and proceed with either.

## Step 3: Normalization

```r
# Proceed with strict QC
passing_cells <- lake$get("qc_strict_cells")$cell_id
filtered_matrix <- lake$get("raw_counts")[, passing_cells]

# Simple log-normalization (in practice, use scran/Seurat)
size_factors <- colSums(filtered_matrix) / median(colSums(filtered_matrix))
normalized <- log1p(sweep(filtered_matrix, 2, size_factors, "/"))

lake$put("normalized_counts", as.data.frame(normalized),
         depends_on = c("raw_counts", "qc_strict_cells"))
lake$snap("03_normalized")
```

## Step 4: Feature Selection & Dimensionality Reduction

```r
# Highly variable genes
gene_vars <- apply(as.matrix(lake$get("normalized_counts")), 1, var)
hvg <- names(sort(gene_vars, decreasing = TRUE))[1:500]
lake$put("hvg_list", list(genes = hvg), depends_on = "normalized_counts")

# PCA (store as object)
pca_data <- lake$get("normalized_counts")[hvg, ]
pca_result <- prcomp(t(as.matrix(pca_data)), center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
lake$put("pca_result", pca_result, depends_on = "normalized_counts")

# UMAP embeddings (store as table for easy querying)
umap_coords <- data.frame(
  cell_id = rownames(pca_result$x),
  UMAP1 = pca_result$x[, 1],  # placeholder; use uwot::umap() in practice
  UMAP2 = pca_result$x[, 2]
)
lake$put("umap_coords", umap_coords, depends_on = "pca_result")
lake$snap("04_dimreduction")
```

## Step 5: Clustering --- Compare Resolutions

```r
# Try multiple clustering resolutions
for (k in c(5, 10, 15)) {
  clusters <- kmeans(pca_result$x[, 1:10], centers = k)
  cluster_df <- data.frame(
    cell_id = rownames(pca_result$x),
    cluster = paste0("C", clusters$cluster)
  )
  lake$put(paste0("clusters_k", k), cluster_df,
           depends_on = "pca_result")
}
lake$snap("05_clustering")

# Compare cluster distributions
for (k in c(5, 10, 15)) {
  cat(sprintf("k=%d: ", k))
  print(table(lake$get(paste0("clusters_k", k))$cluster))
}
```

## Step 6: Cell Type Annotation

```r
# Annotate clusters (k=10 chosen after evaluation)
annotations_v1 <- data.frame(
  cluster = paste0("C", 1:10),
  cell_type = c("T cells", "B cells", "Monocytes", "NK cells", "DCs",
                "T cells", "Fibroblasts", "Epithelial", "Macrophages", "Unknown")
)
lake$put("annotations_v1", annotations_v1, depends_on = "clusters_k10")
lake$snap("06_annotated_v1")

# Revised after marker gene validation
annotations_v2 <- annotations_v1
annotations_v2$cell_type[10] <- "Plasma cells"  # Resolved "Unknown"
lake$put("annotations_v2", annotations_v2, depends_on = "clusters_k10")
lake$snap("06_annotated_v2")
```

## Full Lineage

```r
lake$tree("annotations_v2")
# annotations_v2
#   └── clusters_k10
#         └── pca_result
#               └── normalized_counts
#                     ├── raw_counts
#                     └── qc_strict_cells
#                           └── cell_metadata
```

## Export Results

```r
# Final annotated cell metadata
lake$ref("umap_coords") |>
  dplyr::left_join(lake$ref("clusters_k10"), by = "cell_id") |>
  dplyr::left_join(lake$ref("annotations_v2"), by = "cluster") |>
  save_as("final_cell_annotations", lake)

lake$export("final_cell_annotations", "results/cell_annotations.parquet")
lake$snap("07_final")
```
